evidence_quotes.quote: At the moment any company selling games can terminate the access we have to our purchases. At any time.

paragraphs.text: Hello EU Commision, I would like to see that the Digital Fairness Act also includes video games. With the recent rise of digital gaming a lot of games are going to disappear forever. At the moment most games cost around 70-90 Euro's without using third party websites. And we as a consumer are not buying a game. We are buying a license. At the moment any company selling games can terminate the access we have to our purchases. At any time. We as gamers are not asking to get support for our games until the day we die. We are asking that the company that brings ut the game gives us consumers a last update so we can keep playing the games that we love and bought. So we as enthousiasts of our hobby can make our own servers to play on. We are not asking the company to be fully reliable for their game after they stop supporting it. We understand that that is an unreasonable demand and we accept all the consequences that are linked to this. We just want to keep playing the games that we love. Also we understand that not all games are created equal and a lot of games will still fade away. But they will fade away in the hands of the fans of the game. Who enjoyed every second of play time we had in it. I included a Wiki with a list of games that are already unplayable or about to be. https://stopkillinggames.wiki.gg/wiki/Dead_game_list The other reason I want you to take notice is that this will be a stepping stone for other industries to implement the exact same practices currently going on in the video game industries. It is already happening in the mobile phone sector. And other industries could use this as an excuse to do the exact same thing. as an example, cars are using more and more computer parts. Especially electric cars. Now a car manufacturer could say that for car model X they are going to stop supporting the software used in their cars in X years. Which is basically saying all liability would fall on the consumer for not getting a new car. Yes, I know it sounds far fetched. But when millions of Euro's are involved. People will try everything. Thank you for reading this. Y. Brokking
